pi_items.source: Task description explicitly instructs the agent to save the file exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/dives.db'.
Directly parsing the SampleDivesV2.ssrf XML file (downloaded from the official Subsurface repo) reveals exactly 29 <dive> elements.
setup_task.sh explicitly copies the original data file to this path and launches Subsurface.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real dive data from the official Subsurface SampleDivesV2.ssrf file. The instructions require saving the file to '/home/ga/Documents/dives.db'. However, parsing the underlying SampleDivesV2.ssrf file confirms it contains exactly 29 dives, contradicting the metadata claim of 8. Furthermore, the task instructs the agent to select an 'SQLite sqlite3' format filter in the Subsurface 'Save As' dialog, but Subsurface natively only supports saving as XML (*.ssrf, *.xml) and has no SQLite export functionality. Because the verifier expects a valid SQLite file with a 'dives' table containing exactly 8 records, the task is impossible to complete programmatically.
